    Iteration Plan for January 2026 #286040

    New issue
    Copy link
    New issue
    Copy link
    Closed
    Closed
    Iteration Plan for January 2026#286040
    Copy link
    Assignees
    Labels
    iteration-planVS Code - Upcoming iteration planVS Code - Upcoming iteration plan
    Milestone
    January 2026

    Description

    kieferrm
    opened on Jan 6, 2026 · edited by kieferrm
    Member
    Issue body actions

    This plan captures our work in January 2026. This is a 4-week iteration. We will ship in early February.

    Endgame

    • January 26, 2026: Endgame begins
    • January 30, 2026: Endgame done

    The endgame details for this iteration are tracked here.

    Plan Items

    Below is a summary of the top level plan items.

    Legend of annotations:

    Mark Description
    🏃 work in progress
    ✋ blocked task
    💪 stretch goal for this iteration
    🔴 missing issue reference
    🔵 more investigation required to remove uncertainty
    ⚫ under discussion within the team
    ⬛ a large work item, larger than one iteration

    Accessibility

    • Accessibility issues, see query @meganrogge team
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Workbench

    • Glob matching should be case insensitive vscode#10633 @dmitrivMS
      Investigate improvements for Simple Browser vscode#277298 @kycutler @jruales @unthinkmedia
      Integrated Browser V1 vscode#286579 @kycutler @jruales @unthinkmedia
      Rollout Windows 11 Explorer context menu item to Stable vscode#204696 @deepak1556
      Rollout improved Windows update flow to Stable vscode#281963 @deepak1556
      🏃 Support in-context Quick Pick interactions vscode#238095 @lszomoru @TylerLeonhardt
      🏃 Portable mode improvements @dmitrivMS
      🏃 Metered network connections support @dmitrivMS
      🏃 Update UI improvements @dmitrivMS
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Code Editor

    • Hover on keyboard modifier should trigger instantly vscode#276558 @benvillalobos
      Fix important self host issues with experimental GPU acceleration project @Tyriar
      🏃 Allow configuring font family, font size and line height via syntactic tokens vscode#263545 @aiday-mar
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Languages

    Python

    • Use environment extension api when executing Python code and file vscode-python#25687 @anthonykim1
      • Create terminal api does not return activated terminal for command type vscode-python-environments#1078 @anthonykim1
      • Ctrl+c immeditately issued upon run, execution cancelled by activation command vscode-python-environments#640 @anthonykim1
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.
      • 🏃 Various bug fixes for Python Environments Extension vscode-python-environments-query @eleanorjboyd
    To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Terminal

    • Remove winpty support node-pty#842 vscode#284519 @anthonykim1
      Implement kitty keyboard protocol vscode#286809 @Tyriar
      Implement win32 input mode vscode#286896 @Tyriar
    • ✋ 🏃 Conpty issues @anthonykim1
      • Ship newer version of conpty vscode#224488 @anthonykim1 @Tyriar
      • Issues from conpty on Windows 25H2 vscode#243584, vscode#241978 vscode#274372 @anthonykim1 @Tyriar
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.
      • 🏃 Implement kitty graphics protocol vscode#286141 @anthonykim1
    To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    API

    • API proposals: query @mjbvz @alexr00
      API finalization: query @mjbvz @alexr00
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Source Control

    • 🏃 Git: Support setup steps for new worktrees vscode#276834 @lszomoru
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    AI

    Chat

    • Allow creating new sessions from chat input vscode#284091 @benibenj
      Chat: Improve model management UI vscode#270143 @sandy081
      Address chat performance problems for longer chats vscode#286610 @roblourens @connor4312
      Render progress as tool calls stream in vscode#278640 @lramos15
      Enable mermaid support by default vscode#271372 @mjbvz
      Improve terminal inside chat view vscode#257468 @meganrogge
      Unify changes in the 'working set' area across agent session types vscode#278654 @connor4312 @lostintangent
      Support tool approval in a hover vscode#280617 @connor4312
      🏃 Polish Agent Sessions UI query @bpasero @lostintangent
      🏃 Chat: Support contributable chat context resources vscode#271104 @alexr00
      🏃 Improve chat response rendering query @justschen @jo-oikawa @hawkticehurst
      🏃 Chat Sessions Controller API @mjbvz vscode#276243
      🏃 Revisit terminal tool running background terminals UX vscode#274550 @Tyriar
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    MCP

    • Support MCP registry url property vscode#278398 @sandy081
      Explore supporting MCP apps vscode#260218 @connor4312
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Inline Chat

    • Make inline chat more discoverable vscode#287105 @jrieken
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    NES / Completions

    • Improve the speed for import NES suggestions vscode#277811 @benibenj @dbaeumer
      Improve rendering of inline completions in small edits scenarios (single characters, punctuation) vscode#284517 @benibenj
      Prefer NES/completions over word suggestions vscode#284076 @benibenj
      Add support for diagnostics to the inline completion prompt vscode#277829 @dbaeumer
      Suggest semantic renames when editing an identifier vscode#289124@dbaeumer
      Add support for patch-based model response format vscode#287115 @ulugbekna @vkrd
      Adopt ICompletionsFetchService for completions vscode#277830 @ulugbekna
      💪 Allow running simulation tests that include the completions model vscode#280344 @ulugbekna @benibenj
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Agents

    • Suggest to install GHPRI from Cloud agent sessions vscode#286168 @alexr00
      Support model-specific tools and tool descriptions vscode#277472 @connor4312 @bhavyaus
      Support model / 3rd party agents in Cloud Agents vscode#286170 @joshspicer
      Polish background agent session UX vscode#287043 @lszomoru @rebornix team
      Sandboxing terminal while running commands through agent vscode#277286 @dileepyavan
      🏃 Support for Claude Agent vscode#266962 @TylerLeonhardt
      🏃 Support repo picker for cloud agents and polish empty workspace experience vscode#286020 @osortega @rebornix
      🏃 CLI integration improvements (custom-agent/model/tools) vscode#286028 @DonJayamanne
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Prompt/Instruction Files and Custom Agents

    • Allow parallel subagent execution vscode#274630 @roblourens
      Improve custom agents vscode#286237 @aeschli @pwang347 @digitarald
      Improve skill lookup vscode#286238 @aeschli @pwang347
      Provide skill lookup debugging vscode#286239 @aeschli @pwang347
      Support GitHub's organization custom instructions vscode#276636 @pwang347 @aeschli
      💪 Improve open editor handoff for Plan agent vscode#277432 @aeschli @pwang347
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Code Review

    • 💪 Notify user when CCR no-ops vscode#277286 @benvillalobos
      💪 Add Chat: Code Review command vscode#276977 @benvillalobos
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Engineering

    • Consume codicons from npm vscode#283823 @alexr00
      Explore distributing .dmg for macOS arm64 vscode#265849 @deepak1556
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Documentation

    • Test-driven development user guide vscode-docs#8951 @ntrogh @digitarald
      Refactor Enterprise article into separate articles vscode-docs#9132 @ntrogh @joshspicer
      💪 Working with large codebases user guide vscode-docs#8932 @digitarald @ntrogh
      To pick up a draggable item, press the space bar. While dragging, use the arrow keys to move the item. Press space again to drop the item in its new position, or press escape to cancel.

    Deferred

      Explore layouts for Chat session + Multi File Diff vscode#260226 @joshspicer
      Update Profiles UI to include AI features vscode#270130 @eli-w-king
      Engineering: ESM restore support for ASAR vscode#228064 @deepak1556
      Explore task-oriented workflow for chat/agent sessions vscode-pr#8076 @mjbvz

      Refine chat motion rendering vscode#287175 @hawkticehurst @justchen @roblourens

    pinned this issue on Jan 6, 2026

    iwangbowen commented on Jan 6, 2026

    iwangbowen
    on Jan 6, 2026
    More actions

    Always looking forward to new updates

    iwangbowen, gleiderr, wotterfree, justwe9517c and palric91iwangbowen, ruji-he-zstk and AlexiaCheniwangbowen and leotmiwangboweniwangboweniwangbowen and annasoraccoiwangbowen and ahtamaraliwangbowen

    OrenMe commented on Jan 6, 2026

    OrenMe
    on Jan 6, 2026
    More actions

    The organization/enterprise custom agents and instructions are a great feature
    I would like to raise suggestion to consider more fine grained ability - one custom instruction per org is a stretch IMO and experience and it's more likely that a sub set of repos that are managed by a group might have common standards over and entire org or enterprise
    Same goes for custom agents
    Commented also on #276636 (comment)

    I think .github-private is nice storage solution but then solution would benefit if it would be decoupled, exposed with an api and allow more ways to attach to granular groups by direct assignment or dynamic rules(repo associated to teams or by metadata tags)
    Also would benefit by collecting stats on which sessions uses which instruction or ran with which custom agents by some ID to measure usage and effectiveness/ROI

    OrenMe
    mentioned this on Jan 7, 2026
    • Provide a better mechanism to share agent files #273817

    ivanm696 commented on Jan 8, 2026

    ivanm696
    on Jan 8, 2026
    More actions

    Revert "Add workflow badges to README

    kieferrm
    changed the title [-]Iteration Plan for January 2026 [DRAFT][/-] [+]Iteration Plan for January 2026[/+] on Jan 12, 2026
    kieferrm
    added
    iteration-planVS Code - Upcoming iteration planVS Code - Upcoming iteration plan
    and removed
    iteration-plan-draftVS Code - Upcoming iteration plan (Draft)VS Code - Upcoming iteration plan (Draft)
    on Jan 12, 2026

    devguyio commented on Jan 19, 2026

    devguyio
    on Jan 19, 2026
    More actions

    I'm taking the liberty to add this comment here for better visibility, apologies if it's better suited somewhere else.

    It seems for the last couple of releases, the latest release wasn't being set, so the latest release keeps showing "November 2025 Recovery" .

    This is breaking any tooling that queries GitHub for the latest release (e.g. flatpak build systems) because the new releases aren't showing up.

    I added this comment to the "December 2025 Recovery 1" release issue .

    ZWQ652 commented on Jan 20, 2026

    ZWQ652
    on Jan 20, 2026
    More actions

    Have you considered using new build tools to refactor the build process (eg: esbuild, rolldown)? The current gulp speed is too slow

    iwangbowen and Ayfri
    microsoft
    deleted a comment from on Jan 22, 2026
    microsoft
    deleted a comment from DevFlex-AI on Jan 27, 2026
    microsoft
    deleted a comment from dad932404-cmd on Jan 27, 2026

    chavezgal commented on Jan 30, 2026

    chavezgal
    on Jan 30, 2026
    More actions

    Los agentes personalizados de la organización/empresa y las instrucciones son una gran característica
    Me gustaría sugerir que se considere una capacidad de grano más fino: una instrucción personalizada por organización es un tramo en mi opinión y experiencia y es más probable que un subconjunto de repositorios administrados por un grupo pueda tener estándares comunes sobre toda la organización o empresa
    Lo mismo ocurre con los agentes de aduanas
    Comentado también en #276636 (comment)

    Creo que .github-private es una buena solución de almacenamiento, pero entonces la solución se beneficiaría si se desacoplara, se expusiera con una API y permitiera más formas de unirse a grupos granulares mediante asignación directa o reglas dinámicas (repositorio asociado a equipos o por etiquetas de metadatos)
    También se beneficiaría de recopilar estadísticas sobre qué sesiones usa qué instrucción o se empetró con qué agentes de aduana por alguna identificación para medir el uso y la efectividad/ROI

    kieferrm
    closed this as completedon Feb 7, 2026
    kieferrm
    unpinned this issue on Feb 7, 2026
